heading: Pilot program for monitoring sexual offenders
label: 20981 Pilot program for monitoring sexual offenders
notes: CodificationSection was formerly classified to , The Public Health and Welfare, prior to editorial reclassification and renumbering as this section.section 16981 of Title 42

Amendments2008—Subsec. (a)(1)(C). , amended subpar. (C) generally. Prior to amendment, subpar. (C) set minimum standards for electronic monitoring units used in the pilot program.Pub. L. 110–400, § 4(a)

Effective Date of 2008 Amendment, , , provided that:Pub. L. 110–400, § 4(b)122 Stat. 4228Oct. 13, 2008“The amendment made by subsection (a) [amending this section] shall apply to grants provided on or after the date of the enactment of this Act [].”Oct. 13, 2008
amendments: Amendments2008—Subsec. (a)(1)(C). , amended subpar. (C) generally. Prior to amendment, subpar. (C) set minimum standards for electronic monitoring units used in the pilot program.Pub. L. 110–400, § 4(a)

Effective Date of 2008 Amendment, , , provided that:Pub. L. 110–400, § 4(b)122 Stat. 4228Oct. 13, 2008“The amendment made by subsection (a) [amending this section] shall apply to grants provided on or after the date of the enactment of this Act [].”Oct. 13, 2008

children.label: i
children.text: carrying out programs to outfit sex offenders with electronic monitoring units; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the employment of law enforcement officials necessary to carry out such programs.

children.heading: Duration
children.label: B Duration
children.text: The Attorney General shall award grants under this section for a period not to exceed 3 years.

children.label: i
children.text: provide a tracking device for each offender that contains a central processing unit with global positioning system; and

children.label: ii
children.text: permit continuous monitoring of offenders 24 hours a day.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Each State, local government, or Indian tribal government desiring a grant under this section shall submit an application to the Attorney General at such time, in such manner, and accompanied by such information as the Attorney General may reasonably require.

children.label: i
children.text: describe the activities for which assistance under this section is sought; and

children.label: ii
children.text: provide such additional assurances as the Attorney General determines to be essential to ensure compliance with the requirements of this section.

children.heading: Innovation
children.label: b Innovation
children.text: In making grants under this section, the Attorney General shall ensure that different approaches to monitoring are funded to allow an assessment of effectiveness.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: There are authorized to be appropriated $5,000,000 for each of the fiscal years 2007 through 2009 to carry out this section.

children.label: A
children.text: assessing the effectiveness and value of this section;

children.label: B
children.text: comparing the cost effectiveness of the electronic monitoring to reduce sex offenses compared to other alternatives; and

children.label: C
children.text: making recommendations for continuing funding and the appropriate levels for such funding.
